audit_conclusion.summary: PR #199 contains patch-unique work but the runtime hardening value has been superseded by later governance work on current main. The branch is stale, broadly divergent, and carries unrelated workflow, media, demo, and artifact changes beyond PB-142.
audit_conclusion.close_recommendation: Close PR #199. Do not rebase, merge, or cherry-pick the branch as-is.
audit_conclusion.preserve_strategy: Preserve only documentation and test ideas in a future clean PB if a current-main coverage audit proves a gap.

superseded_files.reason: PB-142 inline runtime enforcement has been superseded by current gateway runtime enforcement, persistent nonce, runtime revocation, attestation freshness, and execution eligibility wiring.

Most PB-142 deny-path scenarios are now represented by current gateway, persistent nonce, runtime revocation, and runtime enforcement tests.
Fixture updates are stale relative to current runtime attestation and nonce/revocation setup.

Branch hygiene tests have been superseded by PB-306 and PB-308.
Workflow changes are stale relative to later governance CI and branch hygiene updates.
Policy manifest artifacts conflict with current main and are not required for PB-142 closure.

superseded_files.reason: These files are unrelated to the narrow PB-142 runtime-hardening closure decision.

conflict_root_cause.summary: PR #199 is based on an old broad governance branch and not a narrow PB-142 branch. The merge conflicts come from long-lived branch drift across workflows, .gitignore, gateway runtime code, live-pilot fixtures, branch hygiene scripts, policy manifests, demo files, and artifact packs.
conflict_root_cause.risk: Rebasing or merging PR #199 would mix unrelated stale artifacts with current fail-closed runtime enforcement and could introduce duplicate or conflicting enforcement paths.

migration_plan_if_needed: Open a new PB branch from current origin/main.
Rebuild runtime-governance enforcement boundary documentation from current PB-291/PB-304 architecture.
Audit current tests against the preserved PB-142 deny-path ideas.
Add only missing tests, if any, without porting PB-142 gateway logic.
Run focused runtime tests, PR-critical governance tier, py_compile, git diff --check, and conflict marker scan.
final_recommendation: Close PR #199 and preserve only documentation/test concepts through a future clean PB if needed.
